---
title: "Error in Ubuntu and Software Center after Cheese install"
date: 2012-12-25
forum: Installation &amp; Upgrades
---

### Post by ljmartz on 2012-12-25
I am running Ubuntu 12.04 dual boot on an Vista Dell Inspiron 1501 laptop.  Everything was running smoothly until I tried to install Cheese through the Software Center.  After the attempted installation of Cheese, the app would not load and any attempt to open Software Center to load would cause it to crash.  I was able to remove Cheese in terminal mode using the following:

sudo apt-get remove --auto-remove Cheese

Cheese is removed, but Software Center still crashes.  The details of the crash report states that the software fails at the following point:

urs/share/software-center/software-center

I also have a red - in the upper right hand corner of the screen that states that a problem occurred when checking for updates.  When I manually try to check for udates I receive the following error message:

E:Type 'dep' is not known on line 57 in source list /etc/apt/sources.list'

I am a noob and realize that I am in deep doo doo.....at least for me....any assistance will be greatly appreciated.

---

### Post by pardalet on 2012-12-25
The contents of your /etc/apt/souces.list might help to solve your problem. Please type the following command on a terminal and copy/paste the result here in the forums:

```
$ cat /etc/apt/sources.list
```



However, from the error message you quoted I'd bet the problem is on that file's 57th line. If that line begins with **dep**, try changing it with **deb** and do a manual update.

---

### Post by ibjsb4 on 2012-12-25
The doo doo is not that deep here  :)

A little further explanation to what "pardalet" was saying.

To open a terminal:

[https://help.ubuntu.com/community/UsingTheTerminal#Starting_a_Terminal](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/UsingTheTerminal#Starting_a_Terminal)

And if you add "-n" to his command it will number the lines for you.

```
cat -n /etc/apt/sources.list
```Just copy and paste that to terminal and enter.  Then either post the whole thing or post line #57 for further instruction.

---

### Post by pardalet on 2012-12-25
Well, since **ljmartz** talked about having manually checked for updates, I assumed he/she knows at least how to open a terminal.


[QUOTE=ibjsb4]```
cat -n /etc/apt/sources.list
```[/QUOTE]

Thank you, I never knew the cat command had such an option! (never bothered to read the man page, really :P)

---

To open a terminal:

And if you add "-n" to his command it will number the lines for you.

```
cat -n /etc/apt/sources.list
```Just copy and paste that to terminal and enter.  Then either post the whole thing or post line #57 for further instruction.

     1    # deb cdrom:[Ubuntu 12.04 LTS _Precise Pangolin_ - Release i386 (20120423)]/ precise main restricted
     2    
     3    # See [http://help.ubuntu.com/community/UpgradeNotes](http://help.ubuntu.com/community/UpgradeNotes) for how to upgrade to

     5    deb [http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/](http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/) precise main restricted
     6    deb-src [http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/](http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/) precise main restricted
     7    
     8    ## Major bug fix updates produced after the final release of the

    10    deb [http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/](http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/) precise-updates main restricted
    11    deb-src [http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/](http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/) precise-updates main restricted

    15    ## review or updates from the Ubuntu security team.
    16    deb [http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/](http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/) precise universe
    17    deb-src [http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/](http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/) precise universe
    18    deb [http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/](http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/) precise-updates universe
    19    deb-src [http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/](http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/) precise-updates universe

    23    ## your rights to use the software. Also, please note that software in 
    24    ## multiverse WILL NOT receive any review or updates from the Ubuntu
    25    ## security team.
    26    deb [http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/](http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/) precise multiverse
    27    deb-src [http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/](http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/) precise multiverse
    28    deb [http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/](http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/) precise-updates multiverse
    29    deb-src [http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/](http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/) precise-updates multiverse
    30    
    31    ## N.B. software from this repository may not have been tested as
    32    ## extensively as that contained in the main release, although it includes
    33    ## newer versions of some applications which may provide useful features.
    34    ## Also, please note that software in backports WILL NOT receive any review
    35    ## or updates from the Ubuntu security team.
    36    deb [http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/](http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/) precise-backports main restricted universe multiverse
    37    deb-src [http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/](http://us.archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/) precise-backports main restricted universe multiverse
    38    
    39    deb [http://security.ubuntu.com/ubuntu](http://security.ubuntu.com/ubuntu) precise-security main restricted
    40    deb-src [http://security.ubuntu.com/ubuntu](http://security.ubuntu.com/ubuntu) precise-security main restricted
    41    deb [http://security.ubuntu.com/ubuntu](http://security.ubuntu.com/ubuntu) precise-security universe
    42    deb-src [http://security.ubuntu.com/ubuntu](http://security.ubuntu.com/ubuntu) precise-security universe
    43    deb [http://security.ubuntu.com/ubuntu](http://security.ubuntu.com/ubuntu) precise-security multiverse
    44    deb-src [http://security.ubuntu.com/ubuntu](http://security.ubuntu.com/ubuntu) precise-security multiverse
    45

    47    ## 'partner' repository.
    48    ## This software is not part of Ubuntu, but is offered by Canonical and the
    49    ## respective vendors as a service to Ubuntu users.
    50    # deb [http://archive.canonical.com/ubuntu](http://archive.canonical.com/ubuntu) precise partner
    51    # deb-src [http://archive.canonical.com/ubuntu](http://archive.canonical.com/ubuntu) precise partner
    52    
    53    ## This software is not part of Ubuntu, but is offered by third-party
    54    ## developers who want to ship their latest software.
    55    deb [http://extras.ubuntu.com/ubuntu](http://extras.ubuntu.com/ubuntu) precise main
    56    deb-src [http://extras.ubuntu.com/ubuntu](http://extras.ubuntu.com/ubuntu) precise main
    57    dep [http://blognux.free.fr/ubuntu](http://blognux.free.fr/ubuntu) hardy main
    58    deb-src [http://blognux.free.fr/ubuntu](http://blognux.free.fr/ubuntu) hardy main

---

### Post by pardalet on 2012-12-25
There you go. :D

Edit /etc/apt/sources.list with your favorite text editor and change that **dep** for a **deb**. For instance:

```
$ sudo gedit /etc/apt/sources.list
```

---

### Post by pardalet on 2012-12-25
On second thoughts, if you say you uninstalled Cheese and you're not using any other webcam program, I think you could just as well delete (or better comment) the last two lines.


PS: To comment a line just edit the file (see my previous post) and put a **#** symbol at the beginning of that line.

---

> **pardalet said:**

Edit /etc/apt/sources.list with your favorite text editor and change that **dep** for a **deb**. For instance:

```
$ sudo gedit /etc/apt/sources.list
```

### Post by ibjsb4 on 2012-12-25
Line 57 and 58 are for Hardy 8o4, need to remove them, your running Precise 12o4.

The GUI way is:

[https://help.ubuntu.com/community/Repositories/Ubuntu#Removing_.26_Disabling_Repositories](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/Repositories/Ubuntu#Removing_.26_Disabling_Repositories)

> **pardalet said:**
> There you go. :D

Edit /etc/apt/sources.list with your favorite text editor and change that **dep** for a **deb**. For instance:

```
$ sudo gedit /etc/apt/sources.list
```

### Post by ibjsb4 on 2012-12-25
I run Classic and its a bit different from Unity, but I think you will find software sources in Dash.  If not, just open it from terminal:

```
gksudo software-properties-gtk
```

---

### Post by ljmartz on 2012-12-25
> **ibjsb4 said:**
> I run Classic and its a bit different from Unity, but I think you will find software sources in Dash.  If not, just open it from terminal:

```
gksudo software-properties-gtk
```

Thanks, that got the GUI software sources up.  Just wondering, if I disable the repositories for Independent and Independent source code, won't that disable all third party software?  I am running third party code so that I can use the DVD player, don't wan't to break the DVD player while trying to fix the Software Center.

### Post by ibjsb4 on 2012-12-25
[https://help.ubuntu.com/community/Repositories/Ubuntu#Removing_.26_Disabling_Repositories](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/Repositories/Ubuntu#Removing_.26_Disabling_Repositories)

And just uncheck (do not remove) lines 57 & 58.  Now try the software center.  And make sure your on the right tab in software sources, you only want to uncheck:

57    dep [http://blognux.free.fr/ubuntu](http://blognux.free.fr/ubuntu) hardy main
    58    deb-src [http://blognux.free.fr/ubuntu](http://blognux.free.fr/ubuntu) hardy main

---

> **ibjsb4 said:**
> [https://help.ubuntu.com/community/Repositories/Ubuntu#Removing_.26_Disabling_Repositories](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/Repositories/Ubuntu#Removing_.26_Disabling_Repositories)

And just uncheck (do not remove) lines 57 & 58.  Now try the software center.  And maksure your on the right tab in software sources, you only want to uncheck:

57    dep [http://blognux.free.fr/ubuntu](http://blognux.free.fr/ubuntu) hardy main
    58    deb-src [http://blognux.free.fr/ubuntu](http://blognux.free.fr/ubuntu) hardy main

Wish my "Other Software" tab listed line 57 & 58.  It doesn't.  Screen shot attached as file.

### Post by ljmartz on 2012-12-25

### Post by ibjsb4 on 2012-12-25
Ok, now I understand the confusion.  Its not showing up there and you need to hash (#) it out using gedit.  A hash mark will do the same as unchecking it.  So ..

```
gksudo gedit /etc/apt/sources.list
```and change

deb [http://blognux.free.fr/ubuntu](http://blognux.free.fr/ubuntu) hardy main
    deb-src [http://blognux.free.fr/ubuntu](http://blognux.free.fr/ubuntu) hardy main

to

#deb [http://blognux.free.fr/ubuntu](http://blognux.free.fr/ubuntu) hardy main
    #deb-src [http://blognux.free.fr/ubuntu](http://blognux.free.fr/ubuntu) hardy main

Then once again try the software center.

You can add line numbers to gedit in Edit>Preferences>Display line numbers

---

### Post by ljmartz on 2012-12-25
Yeah!  Software Center is back.  Still have red - button at top right of screen.  Do I need to reboot to get rid of that?

---

### Post by ibjsb4 on 2012-12-25
When adding third party sources in the future you need to make sure its for precise 12o4.

Go ahead and try a reboot, but Im thinking that should of fixed it.  If after reboot you still get the red button ..

```
sudo apt-get update
```

And please post the output in code tags.

[ATTACH]229153[/ATTACH]
